points.text: Lyora Therapeutics launched with $2.5M pre-seed funding to advance a pipeline of genetic medicines for inherited retinal diseases, including LYA-102 which utilizes optimized CRISPR technology.

Lyora Therapeutics — LYA-102 — Usher Syndrome Type 2 — Utilizes optimized CRISPR technology to address mutations in exon 13 of the USH2A gene; Editas Medicine granted exclusive option to license rights.

Lyora Therapeutics — LYA-102 — Usher Syndrome Type 2 — Program uses optimized CRISPR technology to address exon 13 mutations in the USH2A gene; Editas Medicine granted exclusive option to license USH2A gene editing rights.

Lyora Therapeutics — CRISPR-based gene editing for inherited retinal diseases — LYA-102 (USH2A) + LYA-101 (PRPF31) — $2.5M pre-seed funding secured; IND submission for LYA-101 planned within 18 months.

Lyora Therapeutics — CRISPR-based gene editing for retinal diseases — LYA-102 (USH2A) and LYA-101 (PRPF31) — Raised $2.5M pre-seed; plans IND submission for LYA-101 within 18 months, with LYA-102 to follow.

LYA-102 — Mechanism: CRISPR-mediated gene editing targeting exon 13 of USH2A — Phase: Pre-clinical — Population: Usher Syndrome Type 2 (hearing and vision loss) — Status: Exclusive licensing option secured from Editas Medicine.

LYA-102 — Mechanism: CRISPR-mediated gene editing targeting exon 13 of USH2A — Phase: Pre-clinical — Population: Usher Syndrome Type 2 (potential hearing loss application) — Status: Exclusive licensing option secured from Editas Medicine.

Lyora Therapeutics / Editas Medicine — CRISPR licensing agreement — USH2A gene editing rights — Editas granted Lyora exclusive option to license certain rights applicable to USH2A gene editing for LYA-102.
Lyora Therapeutics — Secured exclusive option from Editas Medicine to license rights for USH2A gene editing technology — Deal supports development of LYA-102 for Usher Syndrome Type 2.
